name: Lajes Field (Azores)
tagline: A mid-Atlantic Air Force stopover base on a Portuguese island in the Azores.
_note: True OCONUS (Azores, Portugal). OHA housing, DoDEA Europe schools (Lajes), command-sponsorship/SOFA notes, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy. Lajes Field (65th Air Base Group) is a strategic mid-Atlantic stopover/refueling base on Terceira Island; drawn down in recent years but active, with a smaller accompanied community. Medical: 65th Medical clinic; complex care via medevac/host-nation. DoDEA Europe (Lajes Elementary/High School). Facts verified from official sources (lajes.af.mil, TRICARE, DoDEA Europe, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx); community size has fluctuated with drawdowns.

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (on a Portuguese base)
data.Role: Strategic mid-Atlantic stopover, refueling, and support
data.Tour type: Accompanied (command-sponsored); a smaller, remote island community
data.Setting: A green Atlantic island, isolated but scenic

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Lajes Field is a strategic U.S. Air Force stopover and support base on Terceira Island in the Azores, a Portuguese island chain in the middle of the Atlantic, long valued as a refueling and transit point between North America and Europe.
It's a smaller, remote, and scenic island tour. The base drew down in recent years, so the American community is modest, but the green-island setting and Portuguese culture are a draw.

data.subtitle: Lajes is small and isolated in the mid-Atlantic. The main choice is on-base housing versus renting on the Portuguese economy with OHA, with island logistics the big factor.
intro: Most families come command-sponsored and choose between on-base housing and renting on the Portuguese economy with the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA).
The big adjustments are the isolation (everything arrives by ship or plane), a smaller community, and the weather, balanced against a beautiful green island and an authentic Azorean experience.

recommendations.advice: On-base housing keeps you close to the small community's schools and services.
Renting on the economy on Terceira offers a deeper Azorean experience, with OHA to offset rent.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and approves economy rentals, and helps with leases and OHA, so start there.
Budget for shipping, limited shopping, and travel that means a flight; lean into the outdoors.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Lajes is a Portuguese base hosting U.S. forces; access follows host-nation and U.S. rules. Visitors without a DoD ID must be sponsored and registered.
Requirements can change, so confirm the current details on the official Lajes Field site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base & OHA)
data: Lajes has on-base family housing, and families may also rent on the Portuguese economy on Terceira, receiving the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and approves economy rentals; it also helps with leases, utilities, and OHA. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of U.S. personnel attend the Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) school at Lajes, serving the community from elementary through high school.
Confirm current school details and registration through DoDEA Europe and the base, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Care: Outpatient primary care on base; specialty, inpatient, and complex care via host-nation hospitals and medical evacuation off-island

intro: An overseas PCS to a remote Atlantic island has extra steps, including shipping and travel planning. Get these sorted early.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: Make sure your family is command-sponsored before or upon arrival; it drives your OHA, DoDEA enrollment, and travel entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get overseas ID and logistics cards as required.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose on-base housing or get approval to rent on the economy with OHA; claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) while you look.

steps.title: Plan your vehicle and travel
steps.text: Arrange vehicle shipping early (island logistics take time), handle local registration, and plan for flights to leave the island. Confirm whether family members need a no-fee (official) passport.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Lajes school early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program; know that complex care can mean medevac off-island, so understand the referral process.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Lajes?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for economy rentals, or you live in on-base housing. OHA is based on your actual rent up to a cap, not the stateside BAH tables.
On Terceira Island in the Azores, a Portuguese island chain in the mid-Atlantic, long used as a strategic refueling and transit base.
Either in on-base housing or on the Portuguese economy on Terceira using OHA.
Small, remote, and scenic. Island logistics (shipping, limited shopping, flights to leave) are real, balanced against a beautiful green island and Azorean culture.
